label: Zendesk
description: Connect to Zendesk to read, create, and update support tickets and customer records.

form.title: Connect Zendesk
form.subtitle: Link Anton to your Zendesk account to manage tickets and customer conversations.

methods.label: API Token
methods.description: Paste your Zendesk API token along with your login email and subdomain. No OAuth setup required — generate a token in Admin Center in under a minute.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Zendesk API Token

### Steps

1. Log in to your Zendesk account and open **Admin Center** (the cog icon in the left sidebar).
2. Navigate to **Apps and integrations → APIs → Zendesk API**.
3. Make sure **Token Access** is enabled (toggle it on if not).
4. Click **Add API token**.
5. Give the token a description like `Anton` so you can identify it later.
6. Copy the token — **it is only shown once**. If you lose it, you'll need to create a new one.
7. Paste the token into the **API Token** field below.
8. Enter the **email address** you use to log in to Zendesk.
9. Enter your **subdomain** — the part before `.zendesk.com` in your Zendesk URL (e.g. `yourcompany` from `yourcompany.zendesk.com`).

### Notes

- The token is scoped to the permissions of the user whose email you provide. Use an admin email for full access.
- You can revoke the token at any time in Admin Center → Apps and integrations → APIs → Zendesk API.
- If your Zendesk instance uses SSO or restricts API access, check with your Zendesk admin.

fields.name: subdomain
fields.label: Zendesk Subdomain
fields.description: The part before .zendesk.com in your Zendesk URL. E.g. for yourcompany.zendesk.com, enter yourcompany.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Login Email
fields.description: The email address you use to sign in to Zendesk. The API token will act with this user's permissions.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Generated in Admin Center → Apps and integrations → APIs → Zendesk API. Shown only once — copy it before closing the dialog.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Use your own Zendesk OAuth client to authorize Anton via a browser consent flow. Requires setting up an OAuth client in Admin Center first.
methods.how_to:
## Set Up OAuth 2.0 for Zendesk

### Create an OAuth Client in Zendesk

1. Log in to Zendesk and open **Admin Center**.
2. Navigate to **Apps and integrations → APIs → OAuth clients**.
3. Click **Add OAuth client**.
4. Fill in:
   - **Client name**: `Anton`
   - **Redirect URL**: `http://localhost` (or the redirect Anton provides)
5. Save the client. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown on the page.

### Configure Anton

1. Enter your **Zendesk subdomain** (e.g. `yourcompany` from `yourcompany.zendesk.com`).
2. Paste the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the OAuth client you just created.
3. Click **Submit**. A browser window will open asking you to authorize Anton.
4. After approval, Anton stores the access token securely.

### Notes

- Scopes requested: `read` and `write`.
- You can revoke access in Admin Center → Apps and integrations → APIs → OAuth clients.

fields.name: subdomain

fields.label: OAuth Client ID

fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
